justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Both training and serving pipelines are fully designed with clear components and data flow. (2) 100ms P99 constraint is addressed as a hard constraint on the serving path—ONNX in-memory inference, pre-computed features via Redis, 80ms hard timeout with heuristic fallback. (3) Feature Store explicitly identified as necessary with online/offline split and shared kernel to prevent train-serve skew. (4) Model versioning and rollback designed via Model Registry with immutable versioned artifacts and alias-based instant rollback. (5) Team size factored throughout—deliberately chose simpler in-memory serving over TFServing/Triton, Redis over Feast/Tecton, weekly batch over continuous learning, all explicitly justified for 4-person team capacity. (6) Trade-offs and risks are thorough and well-reasoned.

observations.text: Exceptionally detailed serving pipeline design with ONNX Runtime, Redis feature store, and heuristic fallback—goes beyond minimum requirements
Feature store stale data risk explicitly called out (zone averages baked into model artifact as fallback), directly matching expected risk identification
Model drift risk addressed via automated Validation Gate with hard thresholds and prediction distribution shift alerting
Shared Kernel pattern for feature definitions prevents train-serve skew—a sophisticated architectural choice
Phased implementation plan starts with heuristic model to get integration live immediately, replacing with ML model later—pragmatic for small team
Trade-off analysis explicitly considers and rejects heavier alternatives (TFServing, Feast, Tecton, SageMaker, A/B platform) with clear reasoning tied to team size

A/B testing capability was explicitly traded away in favor of offline validation only—rubric expected A/B or blue/green deployment capability, though the alias-based rollback provides blue/green equivalent
Rigidity points section is unusually strong—explicitly names synchronous prediction path and ONNX format as intentional constraints, showing deep architectural reasoning

agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Architecture for a real-time ML delivery time estimation service embedded in the checkout critical path. The design separates concerns into three bounded contexts—Feature Engineering, Model Lifecycle, and Prediction Serving—optimized for a 4-person team. The serving layer uses a pre-loaded in-memory model behind a lightweight inference API to hit sub-100ms P99. Model training runs offline on a weekly schedule with automated validation gates and one-click rollback via a mode
